title: Codex critique: graph view UX (ouroboros pass)
body_text: # Codex critique: graph view UX (ouroboros pass) You are reviewing the LLM-Wiki 3D/2D graph view. The user has filed many rounds of complaints; the agent has shipped many fixes; the user still says "3d graph viewing experience is still not so good". Your job: tell us **what's still broken** as a third party. ## Setup - Repo: `/Users/neo/Developer/Projects/LLM-Wiki`. You are already there. - The graph view source: `llm_wiki/site/js.py` (`JS_BUNDLE_GRAPH`), `llm_wiki/site/pages.py::render_graph_view`, `llm_wiki/site/tokens.py` (`.graph-page` CSS). - Live compiled output: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/index.html`, `.llm-wiki/site/assets/graph-.js`. - Graph payload: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-core.json` (top 80 nodes by degree, ~88 KB) + `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-rest.json` (rest, ~253 KB). - The user's recent complaints are listed in commit messages from the last ~50 commits — read `git log --oneline -50` for context. Key parameters in the current implementation (don't change them; just diagnose): - Initial camera at `z=1000`. `OrbitControls.maxDistance = 1200`, `minDistance = 35`. - `nodeRelSize = 6`. Node `val = 2 + min(in_degree, 200)**0.92 * 1.4` so a 30-incoming hub renders at val

frontmatter.summary: # Codex critique: graph view UX (ouroboros pass) You are reviewing the LLM-Wiki 3D/2D graph view. The user has filed many rounds of complaints; the agent has shipped many fixes; the user still says "3d graph viewing experience is still not so good". Your job: tell us **what's still broken** as a third party. ## Setup - Repo: `/Users/neo/Developer/Projects/LLM-Wiki`. You are already there. - The graph view source: `llm_wiki/site/js.py` (`JS_BUNDLE_GRAPH`), `llm_wiki/site/pages.py::render_graph_view`, `llm_wiki/site/tokens.py` (`.graph-page` CSS). - Live compiled output: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/index.html`, `.llm-wiki/site/assets/graph-.js`. - Graph payload: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-core.json` (top 80 nodes by degree, ~88 KB) + `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-rest.json` (rest, ~253 KB). - The user's recent complaints are listed in commit messages from the last ~50 commits — read `git log --oneline -50` for context. Key parameters in the current implementation (don't change them; just diagnose): - Initial camera at `z=1000`. `OrbitControls.maxDistance = 1200`, `minDistance = 35`. - `nodeRelSize = 6`. commands_run: nl -ba llm_wiki/site/pages.py | sed -n '2320,2445p' && nl -ba llm_wiki/site/tokens.py | sed -n '1490,1575p'

python - <<'PY'
import json, math, collections
for name in ['payload-core.json','payload-rest.json','payload.json']:
    p=json.load(open(f'.llm-wiki/site/graph/{name}'))
    nodes=p.get('nodes',[]); links=p.get('links',p.get('edges',[]))
    deg=collections.Counter()
    indeg=collections.Counter()
    groups=collections.Counter(n.get('group') for n in nodes)
    ids={n.get('id') for n in nodes}
    for l in links:
        s=l.get('source'); t=l.get('target')
        if isinstance(s,dict): s=s.get('id')
        if isinstance(t,dict): t=t.get('id')
        deg[s]+=1; deg[t]+=1; indeg[t]+=1
    vals=[]
    for n in nodes:
        vals.append((n.get('val'), n.get('degree'), deg[n.get('id')], indeg[n.get('id')], n.get('name') or n.get('id')))
    vals_sorted=sorted(vals, key=lambda x: (x[0] or 0), reverse=True)[:10]
    print('\n',name, 'nodes',len(nodes),'links',len(links),'groups',groups)
    print('degree max/median', max([v[2] for v in vals] or [0]), sorted([v[2] for v in vals])[len(vals)//2] if vals else 0)
    print('val top', vals_sorted[:8])
    missing=sum(1 for l in links if (l.get('source') if not isinstance(l.get('source'),dict) else l.get('source',{}).get('id')) not in ids or (l.get('target') if not isinstance(l.get('target'),dict) else l.get('target',{}).get('id')) not in ids)
    print('missing endpoint links', missing)
PY

python3 - <<'PY'
import json, math, collections
for name in ['payload-core.json','payload-rest.json','payload.json']:
    p=json.load(open(f'.llm-wiki/site/graph/{name}'))
    nodes=p.get('nodes',[]); links=p.get('links',p.get('edges',[]))
    deg=collections.Counter(); indeg=collections.Counter()
    groups=collections.Counter(n.get('group') for n in nodes)
    ids={n.get('id') for n in nodes}
    for l in links:
        s=l.get('source'); t=l.get('target')
        if isinstance(s,dict): s=s.get('id')
        if isinstance(t,dict): t=t.get('id')
        deg[s]+=1; deg[t]+=1; indeg[t]+=1
    vals=[]
    for n in nodes:
        vals.append((n.get('val'), n.get('degree'), n.get('in_degree'), deg[n.get('id')], indeg[n.get('id')], n.get('name') or n.get('id')))
    vals_sorted=sorted(vals, key=lambda x: (x[0] or 0), reverse=True)[:10]
    print('\n'+name, 'nodes',len(nodes),'links',len(links),'groups',groups)
    print('payload degree max/median', max([v[1] or 0 for v in vals] or [0]), sorted([v[1] or 0 for v in vals])[len(vals)//2] if vals else 0)
    print('local degree max/median', max([v[3] for v in vals] or [0]), sorted([v[3] for v in vals])[len(vals)//2] if vals else 0)
    print('val top')
    for row in vals_sorted[:8]: print(' ', row)
    missing=sum(1 for l in links if (l.get('source') if not isinstance(l.get('source'),dict) else l.get('source',{}).get('id')) not in ids or (l.get('target') if not isinstance(l.get('target'),dict) else l.get('target',{}).get('id')) not in ids)
    print('missing endpoint links', missing)
PY
nl -ba llm_wiki/site/js.py | sed -n '730,815p' && nl -ba llm_wiki/site/js.py | sed -n '1315,1550p'

turns.text:
# Codex critique: graph view UX (ouroboros pass)

You are reviewing the LLM-Wiki 3D/2D graph view. The user has filed many rounds of complaints; the agent has shipped many fixes; the user still says "3d graph viewing experience is still not so good". Your job: tell us **what's still broken** as a third party.

## Setup

- Repo: `/Users/neo/Developer/Projects/LLM-Wiki`. You are already there.
- The graph view source: `llm_wiki/site/js.py` (`JS_BUNDLE_GRAPH`), `llm_wiki/site/pages.py::render_graph_view`, `llm_wiki/site/tokens.py` (`.graph-page` CSS).
- Live compiled output: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/index.html`, `.llm-wiki/site/assets/graph-<hash>.js`.
- Graph payload: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-core.json` (top 80 nodes by degree, ~88 KB) + `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-rest.json` (rest, ~253 KB).
- The user's recent complaints are listed in commit messages from the last ~50 commits — read `git log --oneline -50` for context.

Key parameters in the current implementation (don't change them; just diagnose):

- Initial camera at `z=1000`. `OrbitControls.maxDistance = 1200`, `minDistance = 35`.
- `nodeRelSize = 6`. Node `val = 2 + min(in_degree, 200)**0.92 * 1.4` so a 30-incoming hub renders at val ≈ 35.
- Default labels = 11px white text on rgba(0,0,0,0.5) pill, alpha modulated by `degreeImportanceAlpha(node) = 0.18 + sqrt(degree/maxDegree) * 0.82`.
- Focused-node label: 22px, white on rgba(0,0,0,0.78) pill, with camera-distance scaling clamp(1, 20, dist/60).
- Edge labels: 7px white text, no pill, only on edges incident to focus/hover.
- Edge color default: rgba(255,255,255,0.5); incident: rgba(250,204,21,0.5) (yellow). Edge widths: 0.25 default, 0.9 incident, both * camScale.
- Particles only on hover/focus incident edges. Width 0.6, color pure yellow rgb(255,235,59).
- Click-focus tween: 600ms cubic-in-out via `Graph.cameraPosition()`. Auto-browse: 9s dwell + 1400ms inter-stop fly. Right-click unselects.
- Hover suppressed when a node is selected (`focusedNode || pinnedNode || pinnedLink` short-circuits `onNodeHover`).
- Cursor zoom: custom wheel handler that uses canonical before/after raycast translate algorithm with `camera.updateMatrixWorld(true)`. Sign-only step factor 1.015 / 0.985.
- Auto-fit on init: 50ms + 350ms + onEngineStop, all call `Graph.zoomToFit(...)` for 2D and 3D.
- Layout: graph route is `<main class="main main--wide">`, drops the right rai…
I’ll inspect the recent complaint history, graph source, CSS, rendered output, and payload shape, then write the critique directly to the requested markdown file.

The recent history shows a lot of parameter-chasing around camera distance, label sizing, cursor zoom, fit-to-view, and hover/focus behavior. I’m reading the actual implementation now to separate genuinely broken interactions from settings that are merely subjective.

A few contradictions are already visible: comments still describe removed panels while CSS references them, hover is intentionally disabled during focus, and the split payload is merged into a live force simulation without a second fit. I’m checking payload stats and exact CSS line references before writing the review.

turns.text: I have enough evidence to write this now. I’ll keep it focused on user-visible failures and concrete fixes, not broad redesign notes.
The review file is written. I’m doing a quick size check and a brief readback around the headings so the final confirmation has the exact byte count.
